skill.name: pptx-generator
skill.description: Generate, edit, and read PowerPoint presentations. Create from scratch with PptxGenJS (cover, TOC, content, section divider, summary slides), edit existing PPTX via XML workflows, or extract text with markitdown. Triggers: PPT, PPTX, PowerPoint, presentation, slide, deck, slides.
skill.summary: Create and edit PowerPoint presentations using PptxGenJS and XML manipulation.
skill.author: MiniMax-AI

security_audit.summary: All 513 static analysis findings are false positives. The scanner misidentified markdown code fence backticks as Ruby shell execution (413 findings), hex color codes as weak cryptographic algorithms (83+ findings), and common documentation terms as C2 keywords or system reconnaissance. The skill consists entirely of markdown documentation files that provide instructions, code examples, and reference material for PowerPoint generation using PptxGenJS and Python markitdown. No executable code, network exfiltration, or malicious patterns were found after manual review. Minor note: the skill instructs users to run shell commands (node, python) as part of normal operation, which is expected behavior for this type of productivity skill.

low_findings.title: Dynamic Module Loading Pattern
low_findings.description: The compile.js example uses dynamic require() with template literals to load slide modules. While this is an intentional design pattern for the slide compilation workflow, it could theoretically load unexpected files if the slides/ directory is tampered with. This is a normal Node.js pattern and not a security risk in typical usage.

low_findings.title: User-Initiated Shell Commands
low_findings.description: The skill instructs users to execute shell commands including 'python -m markitdown' for text extraction, 'node compile.js' for PPTX generation, and 'zipfile' operations for XML manipulation. These are user-initiated operations in a controlled environment, not automatic execution. This is expected behavior for a productivity skill but should be noted.

content.user_title: Create and edit PowerPoint presentations with AI
content.value_statement: Generate professional PowerPoint presentations from scratch or edit existing templates using PptxGenJS and XML workflows. This skill handles slide creation, design system application, content extraction, and template-based editing without requiring PowerPoint software.
actual_capabilities: Create PowerPoint presentations from scratch using PptxGenJS with cover, table of contents, content, section divider, and summary slides
Edit existing PPTX templates through XML manipulation including adding, deleting, duplicating, and reordering slides
Extract and analyze text content from existing presentations using markitdown
Apply consistent design systems with color palettes, font pairings, and style recipes across all slides
Generate individual slide JS modules and compile them into a final PPTX file
Support bilingual presentations with English (Arial) and Chinese (Microsoft YaHei) fonts
limitations: Requires Node.js and npm for PptxGenJS, and Python for markitdown text extraction
Slide creation from scratch requires running a compile script (node compile.js) in the local environment
XML-based template editing requires understanding of PPTX internal structure and careful handling of slide relationships
The skill provides instructions and code examples but does not execute commands automatically

use_cases.title: Business report generation
use_cases.description: Generate quarterly business reports with charts, multi-column layouts, and consistent corporate branding from a template presentation.

use_cases.title: Academic lecture slides
use_cases.description: Create lecture presentations with varied slide layouts including content slides, section dividers, and summary slides using appropriate academic design palettes.

use_cases.title: Template-based deck production
use_cases.description: Edit existing branded PowerPoint templates by replacing placeholder content while maintaining the original design, formatting, and slide structure.

prompt_templates.title: Create a presentation from scratch
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a PowerPoint presentation about [topic] for [audience]. The presentation should have approximately [number] slides covering [key points]. Use a [style] design style with [color tone] colors.

prompt_templates.title: Edit an existing template
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the file [template.pptx] as a template. Replace the placeholder content with information about [topic]. Keep the original design and slide count, but vary the layouts to avoid repetition.

prompt_templates.title: Extract and analyze presentation content
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract all text content from [presentation.pptx] and provide a summary of the slide structure, key topics, and any placeholder text that needs updating.

prompt_templates.title: Full presentation workflow with design system
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a professional [number]-slide presentation about [topic]. First, select an appropriate color palette and font pairing from the design system. Plan the slide outline with varied layouts. Generate each slide as a JS module, then compile into the final PPTX. Perform QA by extracting text and checking for placeholder content.

output_examples.input: Create a 10-slide pitch deck for a healthcare startup with a modern and wellness color palette
output: Cover slide with title, subtitle, and branded background
Table of contents with 5 section entries
Section divider slides with large typography
Content slides with 2-column layouts, stat callouts, and icon grids
Summary slide with key takeaways and call to action

output_examples.input: Edit template.pptx to replace placeholder content with Q3 sales data
output: Updated slide content with sales figures, charts, and team photos
Preserved original design elements and branding
QA verification confirming no leftover placeholder text

best_practices: Vary slide layouts across the presentation to avoid monotony. Use multi-column layouts, image-text combinations, quote callouts, and stat cards rather than repeating basic title-and-bullet patterns.
Maintain consistent design system application throughout. Use the theme object contract with exactly five keys (primary, secondary, accent, light, bg) and avoid custom color key names.

Always perform QA after generating slides. Extract text with markitdown, check for leftover placeholder text, verify page number badges, and complete at least one fix-and-verify cycle before delivery.
anti_patterns: Repeating the same layout for every slide. This creates monotonous presentations that look AI-generated. Actively seek layout variety across slides.
Using '#' prefix with hex colors in PptxGenJS. Colors must be 6-character hex strings without the hash symbol (use "FF0000" not "#FF0000") to avoid file corruption.
Using async/await in createSlide() functions. The compile.js workflow requires synchronous slide creation and will not await asynchronous operations.

faq.question: What tools do I need to use this skill?
faq.answer: You need Node.js with PptxGenJS installed (npm install pptxgenjs) for creating presentations from scratch, and Python with markitdown (pip install markitdown[pptx]) for extracting text from existing presentations. For template editing, no additional tools are required beyond Python.

faq.question: Can I edit an existing PowerPoint file?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill extracts the PPTX as XML using Python zipfile, modifies slide content and structure directly in the XML, then repacks it into a valid PPTX file. This preserves the original design, fonts, and formatting.

faq.question: How do I choose colors for my presentation?
faq.answer: The skill includes 18 pre-defined color palettes matched to topics and audiences (e.g., Modern & Wellness for healthcare, Business & Authority for corporate reports). Select a palette that matches your presentation topic, or customize using the design system reference.

faq.question: Can I create presentations with Chinese text?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill supports bilingual presentations. Use Microsoft YaHei for Chinese text and Arial for English text. Both fonts can be applied within the same presentation.

faq.question: Why does the skill use one JS file per slide?
faq.answer: Each slide is a separate JS module that exports a createSlide function. This enables parallel generation using subagents, standalone preview of individual slides, and modular compilation into the final presentation.

faq.question: What slide layouts are available?
faq.answer: The skill supports five page types: Cover Page, Table of Contents, Section Divider, Content, and Summary. Content slides support varied layouts including 2-column, 3-column, image-text combos, quote callouts, stat cards, icon grids, and full-bleed backgrounds.
